description: This file controls the recipes for the Horadric Cubetems structure. Any column field name starting with "*" is considered a comment field and is not used by the game.
source: Colonnes du .txt (BKVince 3.2) ; types/descriptions du guide TXT eezstreet-d2rdoc.

columns.name: description
columns.description: This is a reference field to define the cube recipe

columns.name: enabled
columns.description: Boolean field. If equals 1, then the recipe can be used in-game. If equals 0, then the recipe cannot be used in-game.

columns.name: firstLadderSeason
columns.description: Integer field. The first ladder season this cube recipe can be made on (inclusive). If blank or 0 then it is available in non-ladder.

columns.name: lastLadderSeason
columns.description: Integer field. The last ladder season this cube recipe is ladder-only (inclusive). Must be used in conjunction with firstLadderSeason.

columns.name: min diff
columns.description: The minimum game difficulty to use the recipe (0 = All Game Difficulties | 1 = Nightmare and Hell Difficulty only | 2 = Hell Difficulty only)

columns.name: version
columns.description: Defines which game version to use this recipe (0 = Classic mode | 100 = Expansion mode)

columns.name: op
columns.description: Uses a function as an additional input requirement for the recipe

columns.name: param
columns.description: Integer value used as a possible parameter for the "op" function

columns.name: value
columns.description: Integer value used as a possible parameter for the "op" function

columns.name: class
columns.description: Defines the recipe to be only usable by a defined class.

columns.name: numinputs
columns.description: Controls the number of items that need to be inside the cube for the recipe

columns.name: input 1
columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. References an itemtypes.ItemType, item code, uniqueitems.index, sets.index, or one of the modifiers described below

columns.name: input 2
columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. References an itemtypes.ItemType, item code, uniqueitems.index, sets.index, or one of the modifiers described below

columns.name: input 3
columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. References an itemtypes.ItemType, item code, uniqueitems.index, sets.index, or one of the modifiers described below

columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. columns.name: input 7
columns.description: Controls what items are required for the recipe. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add input modifiers by adding a comma "," to the input and using a code. References an itemtypes.ItemType, item code, uniqueitems.index, sets.index, or one of the modifiers described below

columns.name: output
columns.description: Controls an output item. Uses the item's unique code. Users can also add output modifiers by adding a comma "," to the output and using a code. References an itemtypes.ItemType, item code, uniqueitems.index, sets.index, or one of the modifiers described below

columns.name: lvl
columns.description: Forces the output item level to be a specific level. If this field is used, then ignore the .plvl and .ilvl fields.

columns.name: plvl
columns.description: This is a numeric ratio that gets multiplied with the current player's level, to add to the output item's level requirement

columns.name: ilvl
columns.description: This is a numeric ratio that gets multiplied with .input 1 item's level, to add to the output item's level requirement

columns.name: mod 1
columns.description: Controls the output item properties Reference of properties.code or PropertyGroups.code

columns.name: mod 1 chance
columns.description: The percent chance that the property will be assigned. If this equals 0, then the Property will always be assigned.

columns.name: mod 1 param
columns.description: The "parameter" value associated with the listed property (mod). Usage depends on the property function (See properties.func#)

columns.name: mod 1 min
columns.description: The "min" value to assign to the listed property (mod). Usage depends on the property function (See properties.func#)

columns.name: mod 1 max
columns.description: The "max" value to assign to the listed property (mod). Usage depends on the property function (See properties.func#)
